wiki.summary: "Lucid Dreams" (formerly titled "Lucid Dreams (Forget Me)") is a song by rapper and singer Juice Wrld. It was originally released on June 15, 2017 on his 999 EP before being re-released on May 4, 2018 as the second single to his debut album Goodbye & Good Riddance. The song was produced by Nick Mira & SIDEPCE, with the melody of the song sampling the intro of Sting’s 1993 song "Shape Of My Heart". On "Lucid Dreams," Juice WRLD explores the mental anguish someone feels after experiencing a break-up. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Lucid Dreams" (formerly titled "Lucid Dreams (Forget Me)") is a song by rapper and singer Juice Wrld. It was originally released on June 15, 2017 on his 999 EP before being re-released on May 4, 2018 as the second single to his debut album Goodbye & Good Riddance. The song was produced by Nick Mira & SIDEPCE, with the melody of the song sampling the intro of Sting’s 1993 song "Shape Of My Heart". On "Lucid Dreams," Juice WRLD explores the mental anguish someone feels after experiencing a break-up. Lucid dreaming refers to a state of consciousness where a person is aware they are dreaming. With enough experience, a dreamer can slowly begin to control the contents of their dreams. Juice uses the idea of controlling a lucid dream as an analogy for his mental state; to avoid the pain of seeing his ex-lover in his dreams, Juice seeks to replace them—for good. In a June 2018 interview with Lyrical Lemonade, Juice Wrld stated the song was made to be a "therapy session" during a period of relationship problems he was encountering. In the lyrics, he describes the pain of a girl breaking up with him, and in the verse he relates back to the title: saying he has lucid dreams thinking of her. Lucid Dreams was initially released on SoundCloud on June 15, 2017, alongside the EP JuiceWrld 9 9 9. It eventually accumulated 2.5 million plays, leading to an official release as a single on May 4, 2018. In November 2018, producer Nick Mira revealed that Sting owned 85% of the rights to "Lucid Dreams," which features an interpolation of his 1993 hit "Shape of My Heart". Lucid Dreams debuted at #74 on the Billboard Hot 100 on the week ending May 26, 2018, with 10 million US streams and 4,000 digital downloads. The song jumped to #35 the following week and then to #15.When it ascended to #9, Lucid Dreams became Juice Wrld's first top 10 song on the week of June 16, 2018 with over 35.9 million US streams and 9,000 downloads. It slowly climbed to #3 on the chart in July, the song lingered in the top 10 for months until it peaked at #2 for 2 non-consecutive weeks in October 2018, prevented from reaching the top spot by Maroon 5's "Girls Like You" featuring Cardi B. Following Juice Wrld's death in December 2019, "Lucid Dreams" re-entered the Hot 100 at #8, becoming its 26th nonconsecutive week in the top-10. During the 2019 Grammy telecast, host Alicia Keys performed 30 seconds of "Lucid Dreams", singing and playing piano, during a segment on songs about heartache. The official music video for Lucid Dreams was directed by Cole Bennett, released and uploaded on Lyrical Lemonade's YouTube channel on May 11, 2018. It features Juice Wrld trapped up to his head in a floor, with his body tied down, and has been described by as "psychedelic and abstract". The music video reached a billion views on August 7, 2024, and as of 2026, the music video has over 1.1 billion views on YouTube. The song has over 3 billion streams on Spotify, being one of the most-streamed songs on the platform. As of May 23, 2023 the song has been certified 11x platinum by the RIAA. Read more on Last.fm.
